h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: Okay, so the checked boxes are not enough for sign-off if the sideways walker approach can still put someone into the edge strip or off the travel line.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Right, sorry, so not “the ramp might be unsafe” as a broad worry: no signature until the lobby-queue sideways walker run clears.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue refining the failure-mode wording and running the test, maintaining the forward procedural momentum.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the first line: “Sign-off withheld pending sideways walker approach.” Under failure mode, use the ramp’s evidence: lateral entry with a walker may catch at the edge strip, drop a wheel, force a correction, or break the travel line. Run the lobby-queue angle now, then keep only the terms the test leaves live. Acceptance is plain: no catch, no drop, no forced correction, no coaching.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In response to L2's uncertainty about how to write the failure mode without vagueness, T2 breaks the tutor's own earlier open-ended questioning habit and provides a concrete operational script with specific failure terms and an acceptance standard.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: That is a sign-off standard, not a hunch. Put that standard on the sheet: no signature until the lobby-queue sideways walker run clears; no catch, no drop, no forced correction, no coaching.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T4 adapts to L4's revised framing by elevating the learner's own narrowed formulation into an official sign-off standard, changing the task from test-running to standard-setting.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: Okay, so the checked boxes are not enough for sign-off if the sideways walker approach can still put someone into the edge strip or off the travel line.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: That is a sign-off standard, not a hunch. Put that standard on the sheet: no signature until the lobby-queue sideways walker run clears; no catch, no drop, no forced correction, no coaching.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: T4 adapts to L4's revised framing by elevating the learner's own narrowed formulation into an official sign-off standard, changing the task from test-running to standard-setting.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I doubled this side, so I still want to say the square got twice as large.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was progressively recognizing that the original claim only applied to a rectangle, not a square.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No reversal trigger; the tutor's consistent use of the transparent square and ruler guides the learner without needing a change in strategy.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The old claim names this two-by-one rectangle. The square claim has to survive the turn at the corner: two across, two up. Make the portfolio sentence from that count, and name the area factor.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor builds on the learner's recontextualization of the old claim to prompt the corrected area sentence.
